creator: chewi@aura-online.co.uk
text:
Apologies for sounding a little irate here but I have explained the
problem to Rodrigo twice, without going into excessive detail so as not
to insult his intelligence, but he is just not reading what I am
writing.

Yes, libedit compiles. So what? I hope that FreeBSD doesn't have a Q/A
policy of "if it compiles, ship it". The problem is that it is not
linking to one of the libraries that define tgetent and friends, thus
you end up with a libedit that has unresolved symbols.

This forces other users of the library (like FreeSWITCH) to explicitly
link against one of these libraries when it would otherwise not be
necessary to do so.

I was curious to see how the FreeBSD dependencies of libedit deal with
this so I had a look at lang/onyx. It applies this ugly workaround.

AC_CHECK_LIB([termcap], [tgetent], [MODPROMPT_LDFLAGS="-ltermcap"],
  [AC_CHECK_LIB([tinfo], [tgetent], [MODPROMPT_LDFLAGS="-ltinfo"],
    [AC_CHECK_LIB([curses], [tgetent], [MODPROMPT_LDFLAGS="-lcurses"],
      [AC_CHECK_LIB([ncurses], [tgetent],
                    [MODPROMPT_LDFLAGS="-lncurses"])])])],
  [enable_modprompt="0"])
LIBS="$LIBS $MODPROMPT_LDFLAGS"

This mess could be removed if you apply my tiny patch to libedit. The
version of libedit found in the FreeBSD base system already links
correctly against libncurses so this package should certainly follow
suit. I don't even know why this package exists given that the base
system version appears to be newer.

Regards,
James
